raw_consumer_statement:
Re : Supplemental Statement CFPB Complaint No. XXXX Company : American Express Product XXXX Credit Card To Whom It May Concern, I am submitting this supplemental statement to clarify the record while this complaint remains open and under review, following American Expresss interim response dated XX/XX/year>. 

This submission is intended to ensure that the facts are accurately reflected and that my rights as a consumer are preserved during the CFPBs ongoing review. 

1. Clarification Regarding Fraud Designation I reiterate clearly and unequivocally that the disputed charges on my American Express account were not made or authorized by me. 

At no point did I knowingly or voluntarily withdraw a fraud claim. Any deviation from a fraud designation occurred only after repeated communications in which American Express representatives : Discouraged me from formally pursuing a fraud claim, Minimized the protections available to me, Framed escalation as harmful to my account standing or relationship, And failed to clearly explain the consequences of not maintaining the fraud classification. 

Any suggestion that I abandoned a fraud claim was the result of misleading guidance and pressure, not informed consent. 

2. Misleading and Improper Dispute Handling American Express initially confirmed that the transactions qualified as fraud. However, instead of supporting that determination, the company : Redirected the process away from formal fraud handling, Used my good-faith attempt to resolve the matter amicably against me, Failed to provide transparent explanations of my rights or options. 

I even offered to pay charges that were not mine solely to preserve a long-standing relationship, not as an admission of responsibility. This offer was later treated as justification to deny fraud protections, which is improper and unfair. 

3. Ongoing Harm and Unresolved Issues As of today : Unauthorized charges remain unresolved ; Fraud protections were not properly applied ; I was denied a fair, transparent, and informed dispute process. 

These failures deprived me of the protections afforded to consumers in cases of unauthorized transactions and raise concerns about misleading dispute practices.
